items.title: Markets react to lower lows in WTI crude and mixed PMIs.
items.excerpt: Bob Iaccino of Path Trading Partners breaks down the early sell-off in stock index futures and the continued slide in gold, silver, and copper. He analyzes the mixed manufacturing PMI data from the U.K., Germany, Eurozone, and the U.S. Iaccino also covers WTI crude's seventh lower low in eight sessions amid rocky peace talks and easing supply concerns. Finally, he previews the impact of the latest U.S. Treasury auctions, post-FOMC yield holds, and key upcoming corporate earnings and global economic data.

items.title: ETFs: the lazy investor’s serious tool
items.excerpt: Key takeaways ETFs can help investors spread risk without trying to pick every future winner. Saxo client behaviour suggests diversified investors have often had better odds of profitable outcomes. ETF investing still requires checking what sits inside the basket. Markets have been kind to investors, but not evenly kind. Over the past five years, many major equity indices have moved higher, helped by artificial intelligence, technology and a handful of very large companies. That is good news, unless your portfolio missed the party because it was waiting outside with the wrong invitation. Source: Bloomberg. Chart generated using ASKB by Bloomberg AI. Five-year performance as of 23 June 2026. Returns are shown in local currency terms. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future…

items.title: Metals struggle as markets price peak hawkishness, not peak demand
items.excerpt: Key points: Metals remain under pressure as investors focus on higher funding costs, a stronger dollar and broad-based risk reduction across financial markets. Gold and silver continue to suffer from the prospect of higher-for-longer US rates, despite several long-term supportive drivers remaining intact. Copper has retreated towards key support despite ongoing inventory drawdowns and a constructive medium-term supply outlook. In our opinion we believe markets may be approaching peak hawkishness as falling energy prices ease inflation pressures and reduce the need for additional policy tightening. Industrial and precious metals remain on the defensive as financial markets continue to adjust to the prospect of higher funding costs and a more restrictive US monetary policy outlook. The…

items.title: Euro futures fell to 11-month lows as U.S. yields rose.
items.excerpt: Euro futures fell for a third consecutive session, dropping about a third of a percent to test March lows and approach 11-month lows. This downward pressure comes amid a transition in Federal Reserve leadership and a renewed rise in U.S. Treasury yields, which boosted the U.S. dollar relative to the euro. Additionally, the European Central Bank remains in focus ahead of upcoming policy decisions, while soft Eurozone economic data indicates a slowing growth structure alongside persistent inflation challenges. While the euro faces notable selling pressure, other major FX markets remain relatively quiet, with the British pound gaining slightly and the Japanese yen, Australian dollar, and Canadian dollar trading near unchanged.

items.title: Protecting your gains without selling: how to hedge a tech portfolio with options
items.excerpt: Sitting on significant unrealized gains in tech, but worried the second half of the year could give some back? Options give investors a third path: keep the position, add a floor, and in some cases get paid to do it. This article walks through three practical hedging approaches using one investor's USD 250,000 tech portfolio as the guide. Protecting your gains without selling: how to hedge a tech portfolio with options A USD 250,000 tech portfolio, significant unrealized gains, and a nervous feeling about the months ahead here is how one investor thought about protecting what he had built. (fictional, but educational use-case) Thomas holds a concentrated tech portfolio NVDA, AAPL, MSFT, AMZN, and META making up roughly 75% of his USD 250,000. He still believes in the long-term thesis.…

items.author: Saxo Bank

items.title: British Pound futures slid below 1.32 following hawkish FOMC policy.
items.excerpt: British Pound futures fell below the 1.32 level for the first time since mid-April, accelerating a two-day sell-off of nearly 2%. The move follows a hawkish FOMC meeting that prompted market participants to price in potential rate hikes by the end of the year, strengthening the U.S. dollar. In contrast, the Bank of England kept interest rates unchanged and signaled a patient approach, providing no hawkish counterweight to the Federal Reserve's stance. According to CME Group CVOL data, this divergence caused volatility to spike in the pound, even as volatility declined in equity indices and fixed income markets. Commitment of Traders data highlighted that speculators grew their net short positioning prior to the central bank announcements.
items.author: CME Group

items.title: The FX Trader: USD knee jerks stronger on first Warsh-led FOMC.
items.excerpt: Will USD rally stick beyond the very near term? The latest The FOMC takeaway was quick and sharp as the elimination of the easing bias (and all bias) in the new and stripped-down monetary policy statement by itself was hawkish . The statement itself observed that the economy is doing well, despite the uncertainties from the Middle East . The accompanying SEP economic projections of much higher core and headline inflation and a +0.4% bump in the median Fed rate forecast for this year relative to the March forecast, and +0.6% bump for next year also read as hawkish. Of course, with new Fed Chair Warsh setting in motion a task force to overhaul the Fed s communication strategy and with his specific distaste for forward guidance of the Fed s intentions, we may have seen our last, or…
items.author: John J. Hardy

items.title: Weather risk returns as El Niño threatens crops, grids and mines
items.excerpt: Key points: NOAA has confirmed the arrival of El Ni o, with a growing risk the event strengthens into a very strong episode during the second half of 2026. Agriculture remains the sector most exposed, with weather risks building across several regions, most notably in Asia, while other regions may benefit. Metals face risks through both mine disruption and power shortages, with copper, aluminium, zinc and lithium among the most exposed. Hotter temperatures and reduced hydropower output may boost electricity demand and increase reliance on coal and natural gas across parts of Asia, potentially spilling over to gas prices in Europe. After several years dominated by geopolitical disruptions, trade tensions and shifting monetary policy expectations, weather is once again emerging as a key…

items.title: Euro futures held inside range ahead of Warsh Fed debut.
items.excerpt: Euro futures finished the New York cash session with an inside range day, reflecting market hesitation ahead of major macroeconomic catalysts. Eurozone core inflation provided an upside surprise, reaching 2.6% year-over-year, driven by a jump in services inflation to 3.5%, while the headline rate held steady at 3.2%. Traders are closely monitoring back-to-back central bank decisions, starting with the Federal Reserve meeting under new chair Kevin Warsh. Markets anticipate a steady Fed rate, followed by the Bank of England's decision to maintain its 3.75% base rate. Market participants are focusing heavily on ensuing press conferences and policy statements, which are expected to drive volume and near-term direction for Euro futures against both the dollar and sterling.
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Stronger-than-expected Eurozone core CPI and U.S. retail sales.
items.excerpt: Bob Iaccino breaks down the latest market movements, highlighting mild gains in stocks despite lagging Dow and S P futures. He discusses the stronger-than-expected Eurozone core CPI and U.S. retail sales, noting the impact of inflation on consumer data. The update previews Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting as Fed Chair, emphasizing the importance of the upcoming Summary of Economic Projections and potential shifts in communications. Additionally, Iaccino touches on the Bank of England's expected rate decision and highlights upcoming earnings from Accenture and Kroger.
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Euro futures climbed to a 2-week high on ECB policy focus.
items.excerpt: Euro futures climbed for a 4th consecutive session, reaching a 2-week high of 1.165 as momentum continues to carry forward. Dan Deming from KKM Financial breaks down the primary drivers behind this upward price action, highlighting a softening tone in the Middle East that has put global pressure on energy markets, dragging WTI Crude Oil futures below $80 a barrel. This shift has provided notable support to the foreign exchange markets. Additionally, the Euro continues to find support from last week's ECB announcement, where policymakers elected to raise interest rates to remain vigilant against inflation. While the Euro remains near the lower end of its wider range for 2026, recent price action indicates it has established a higher low relative to the lows established back in March,…
